title: Latent Print Development Technique Selector
description: Apply substrate-porosity logic to select the correct latent fingerprint development technique. Choose an evidence item (glass, paper, cardboard, plastic bag, wood), pick a technique (Cyanoacrylate, Ninhydrin, Silver Nitrate, or Iodine), and apply it; a correct choice develops a print in the technique-accurate color, while a wrong choice shows a 'no visible ridge pattern' failure with the substrate reason.
creator: Forensic Science Intelligent Textbook
subject: Forensic Science
Fingerprint Analysis
Latent Print Development
Dactyloscopy
Evidence Processing
bloomLevel: Apply (L3) — select a development technique by matching it to substrate porosity.

educational.topic: Selecting a latent print development technique based on substrate porosity
learningObjectives: Apply the rule that porous substrates take chemical reagents (Ninhydrin, Silver Nitrate) while non-porous substrates take Cyanoacrylate fuming.
Select the correct technique for a given evidence item and predict the developed print color.
Explain why a mismatched technique produces no visible ridge pattern.
educational.prerequisites: Knowledge that fingerprints leave invisible sweat/oil residue; basic idea of porous vs. non-porous materials

pedagogical.interactionPattern: The student selects an evidence item from a dropdown; a before panel shows the substrate with a porous/non-porous badge and a faint invisible latent print. The student chooses one of four technique buttons and presses Apply Technique. The after panel animates the result: a correct technique reveals ridges in the technique-accurate color (white cyanoacrylate, purple ninhydrin, dark silver-nitrate, yellow-brown iodine), while a wrong technique shows 'No visible ridge pattern'. A feedback panel explains the substrate logic and, on failure, why the technique did not work.
pedagogical.assessment: Active choice with consequence: the student must match technique to substrate. Correct choices develop a print; incorrect choices fail with a substrate-specific explanation, teaching porosity logic through error feedback rather than a correct-only demonstration.
pedagogical.modelType: Rule-based substrate model: each technique works on porous, non-porous, or any surface; success is computed by comparing the chosen technique's requirement to the selected item's porosity, then a procedural loop-pattern fingerprint is rendered in the technique's color.
controls: Evidence item dropdown (glass, paper, cardboard, plastic bag, wood)
Four technique buttons (Cyanoacrylate, Ninhydrin, Silver Nitrate, Iodine)
